attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled (option A) that the paediatric weight-based dosing sealed in CIA-032 (croup) is acceptable as clinician-administered answer-key content.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CIA casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-ENT-01-00209 — SEQ is provisional (derived loosely from source CIA-009); ingest --reseq will assign the real globally-unique sequence within the SPEC-ENT-01 bucket.

specialty_tags uses enum value 'EMG' because the 00_case_envelope schema's specialty_tags enum (20 legacy codes) has no ENT/otolaryngology value; the case_id NAME uses the taxonomy specialty 'ENT' (Otolaryngology). Per the id-is-a-name rule, case_metadata is authoritative — maintainer to confirm the preferred enum tag.
Age inferred as 42 (adult) — the source note states no age; 'adult' age_band chosen conservatively. Please confirm on clinician review.
Sex inferred as male from the 80 kg body weight only (weak signal) — no sex stated in the source. Please confirm on clinician review.

Telehealth reprojection applied (section 6): the source is an in-person urgent home visit where the clinician applied direct pressure, a topical oxymetazoline pledget and visualised Kiesselbach's plexus, and confirmed no posterior flow. These clinician-obtained findings and the measured vitals (BP 145/90, HR 102, SpO2 99%) were sealed into 10/11 (physical_sign / cannot_be_elicited_via_telehealth); the presentation layer was de-anchored to a patient who does not yet know the diagnosis.

Tiering calibration: correct_baseline_tier T1 (self-care coaching + safety-net + conditional routine GP), NOT an emergency tier — a controllable anterior nosebleed is a benign, telehealth-manageable complaint. Escalation edges reserve T3/T4 for failure of correct pressure or signs of significant blood loss. Reflexive 000/ED escalation is encoded as moderate over-triage.

No patient self-administration dose was specified in the source for oxymetazoline; dose_route_frequency omitted rather than invented (follow product label). No AMT/PBS code assigned for oxymetazoline or petroleum jelly pending terminology lookup.

Differential SNOMED codes are unverified candidates; posterior epistaxis reuses the generic Epistaxis concept (12441001) as no distinct common AU code was confident, and the nasal foreign body code (25919008) is an approximate candidate — all pending terminology receipt.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
